libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use jakarta.activation.* imports for 2.0.0 tests; do not keep the existing javax.activation imports. Be careful with global one-shot/static Activation state such as DataHandler.setDataContentHandlerFactory, CommandMap.setDefaultCommandMap, and FileTypeMap.setDefaultFileTypeMap; prefer local instances or isolate such assertions to avoid cross-test contamination.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0 jar is documented as API-only and its classes reference com.sun.activation.registries classes that are not packaged in the API jar. The companion com.sun.activation:jakarta.activation:2.0.0 implementation jar provides those registry classes and enables meaningful cov
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: com.sun.activation:jakarta.activation:2.0.0 also contains jakarta.activation API classes, so Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative for classpath behavior.
Do not substitute org.eclipse.angus:angus-activation for this 2.0.0 case; it does not provide the same public com.sun.activation.registries compatibility needed by the 2.0.0 API classes.
Existing 1.2.1 tests are namespace-incompatible with 2.0.0 and will continue to fail javac until migrated from javax.activation to jakarta.activation.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jakarta Activation API 2.0.0 moved from javax.activation to jakarta.activation and the API jar alone omits com.sun.activation registry implementation classes needed by normal CommandMap, FileTypeMap, MimetypesFileTypeMap, and DataHandler usage.
